core_mechanism: universal corrections-to-scaling near a critical/continuum limit driven by a marginally irrelevant coupling, so that the leading deviations of a discretized system from its limiting behaviour are independent of microscopic details yet decay only as inverse powers of the logarithm of a diverging correlation length rather than as a power law
brief_reason: The abstract's central result is that the leading lattice artifacts of the continuum limit are universal but vanish only as inverse powers of the logarithm of the correlation length, which is the hallmark of logarithmic corrections-to-scaling generated by a marginally irrelevant operator.
